# Jarvy — Full LLM Reference

> Single-file flat reference for AI agents. Optimized for one-shot context injection. For human-readable docs see https://jarvy.dev. For interactive AI integration use the MCP server (`jarvy mcp`).

Jarvy is a fast, Rust-based, cross-platform CLI that provisions local development environments from a declarative `jarvy.toml` config file. It runs on macOS, Linux, and Windows, using each platform's native package managers (Homebrew, apt, dnf, pacman, apk, winget, Chocolatey, Scoop). It is MIT/Apache-2.0 licensed and hosted at https://github.com/bearbinary/jarvy.

This file is the canonical machine-consumption reference. It mirrors `https://jarvy.dev` but flattened for easy ingestion.

---

## 1. Installation

```bash
# Cargo
cargo install jarvy

# Homebrew (macOS, Linux)
brew install jarvy

# Direct download
# https://github.com/bearbinary/jarvy/releases
```

Verify:

```bash
jarvy --version
```

### Release Channels

```bash
# Per-install
JARVY_CHANNEL=beta curl -fsSL \
  https://raw.githubusercontent.com/bearbinary/jarvy/main/dist/scripts/install.sh | bash

# Per-update
jarvy update --channel beta

# Persistent (~/.jarvy/config.toml)
[update]
channel = "beta"   # stable | beta | nightly
```

| Channel | Accepts |
|---------|---------|
| stable | `vX.Y.Z` only |
| beta | `vX.Y.Z`, `-rc.N`, `-beta.N` |
| nightly | All including `-alpha.N` |

---

## 2. Quick Start

```toml
# jarvy.toml
[provisioner]
git = "latest"
node = "20"
docker = "latest"
python = "3.12"
```

```bash
jarvy setup
```

Every developer who runs `jarvy setup` gets the same tools and versions.

### Integrating Jarvy Into An Existing Project (LLM workflow)

Canonical first-contact workflow when a user asks an AI assistant in
their own repo to "implement https://jarvy.dev/ into this project" or
similar. End state: a committed `jarvy.toml` (plus optionally
`Makefile` + `scripts/bootstrap.sh`) that any teammate or new laptop
can bring up with one command.

```
Step 1. Confirm Jarvy is installed: `jarvy --version`
        If not, ask the user to install (do NOT auto-install — Jarvy
        runs with elevated privileges, user must opt in):
          curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/bearbinary/jarvy/main/dist/scripts/install.sh | bash
          # or
          cargo install jarvy
          # Windows
          irm https://raw.githubusercontent.com/bearbinary/jarvy/main/dist/scripts/install.ps1 | iex

Step 2. Detect the project's stack from lockfiles / manifests:
          pnpm-lock.yaml          → node-pnpm
          bun.lockb / bunfig.toml → node-bun
          package-lock.json (only) → node-npm
          deno.json / deno.jsonc  → deno
          uv.lock / pyproject [tool.uv] → python-uv
          requirements.txt (no uv) → python-api
          go.mod (HTTP service)   → go-api
          Cargo.toml [workspace]  → rust-workspace
          Cargo.toml single [package] → rust-cli
          Gemfile + config/application.rb → ruby-rails
          pom.xml Spring Boot parent → java-spring
          React/Vite              → react-app
          mixed FE+BE monorepo    → fullstack
          Terraform/Helm/K8s repo → k8s-platform
        Full table: https://jarvy.dev/templates-index/#decision-table

Step 3. Fetch the template into the project:
          TEMPLATE=<picked>
          curl -fsSL "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/bearbinary/jarvy/main/examples/${TEMPLATE}/jarvy.toml" -o jarvy.toml
        Optionally add the one-command bootstrap:
          curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/bearbinary/jarvy/main/Makefile -o Makefile
          mkdir -p scripts
          curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/bearbinary/jarvy/main/scripts/bootstrap.sh -o scripts/bootstrap.sh
          chmod +x scripts/bootstrap.sh

Step 4. Customize versions from the project's actual hint files:
          .nvmrc / package.json engines.node → node = "..."
          .python-version / pyproject requires-python → python = "..."
          go.mod "go X.Y" → go = "..."
          rust-toolchain.toml channel → rust = "..."
          .tool-versions (asdf/mise) → one line per tool
          .ruby-version → ruby = "..."
        Never invent versions. If a hint is missing, keep the template
        default and ask the user.

Step 5. Validate:
          jarvy validate
          jarvy diff
          jarvy setup --dry-run

Step 6. Surface the dry-run plan to the user. Get explicit
        confirmation before any real `jarvy setup`. Ask whether to
        (a) commit config only for teammates to run later, or
        (b) also run `jarvy setup` now to provision this machine.

Step 7. Commit:
          git add jarvy.toml [Makefile scripts/bootstrap.sh]
          git commit -m "feat(devenv): provision via Jarvy"

Step 8. Optional — wire CI:
          jarvy ci-config github   # or: gitlab | circleci | etc.
```

Anti-patterns: do not run `jarvy setup` without `--dry-run` first; do
not auto-commit secrets (use `{ env = "VAR" }` indirection); do not
pin every tool to `latest` (pin at least the major); do not duplicate
tools the registry already has (run `jarvy search <tool>`); do not
bypass roles when teammates need different tool sets.

Full walkthrough with rationale and per-step anti-patterns:
https://jarvy.dev/for-ai-agents/#integration-quickstart-integrate-jarvy-into-this-project

### Real-world integration example (Node.js + bun)

End state of a verified integration into a Node + bun project on
2026-05-12 using the `node-bun` template:

Added:
  jarvy.toml      — provisions git, nvm, node, bun (all "latest").
                    Hooks: corepack enable post-node-install,
                    bun install post-setup.
  .mcp.json       — registers `jarvy mcp` server for Claude Code
                    AI integration.

Modified `package.json` scripts:

  "setup":    "jarvy setup"
  "predev":   "command -v jarvy >/dev/null 2>&1 && jarvy setup --quiet || echo 'jarvy not installed — skipping auto-provision (run bun run setup to install)'"
  "prebuild": "command -v jarvy >/dev/null 2>&1 && jarvy setup --quiet || echo 'jarvy not installed — skipping auto-provision'"

Why graceful skip: bun runs `predev` before `dev` automatically. A
hard failure when jarvy is missing would block contributors who
have not yet installed jarvy and would break CI lanes. The
`command -v jarvy && … || …` form makes auto-provision opt-in by
presence.

Verified:
  - `jarvy validate` → [OK] Configuration is valid! (0 errors,
    0 warnings)
  - `bun run setup` → installed nvm, ran corepack hook after node,
    ran bun install post-setup hook, wrote .jarvy/state.json drift
    baseline.

User flows after integration:
  - New contributor (no jarvy): clones, runs `bun dev`, sees hint,
    runs `bun run setup`, jarvy installs node/bun/nvm/git via
    native package managers + runs bun install, then bun dev works.
  - Existing dev (has jarvy): `bun dev` triggers `jarvy setup
    --quiet` → no-op fast skip when satisfied; drift check passes.
  - CI runner (no jarvy): bun dev / bun run build proceed with hint
    printed; CI installs deps via its own caching path.

This pre<script>+graceful-skip pattern is the recommended way to
wire Jarvy into existing package.json / Makefile / justfile without
forcing every collaborator or CI lane to have Jarvy installed.

### Templates

```bash
jarvy init                          # Interactive
jarvy init --template react
jarvy init --template rust-cli
jarvy init --template python-api
jarvy init --template k8s-admin
jarvy init --template go-api
jarvy init --stdout                 # Print without writing
```

---

## 3. Configuration Reference

### `[provisioner]` — Tools

Simple form:

```toml
[provisioner]
git = "latest"
node = "20"
python = ">=3.10"
```

Detailed form:

```toml
[provisioner]
node = { version = "20", version_manager = true }
python = { version = "3.12", use_sudo = false }
```

| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| `version` | string | required | Version requirement |
| `version_manager` | bool | `true` | Use nvm/pyenv/rustup if available |
| `use_sudo` | bool | auto | Override sudo per-tool |

### Version Operators

| Operator | Example | Meaning |
|----------|---------|---------|
| `latest` | `"latest"` | Any version |
| bare | `"20"` | Major version 20.x.x |
| `>=` | `">=3.10"` | 3.10 or higher |
| `^` | `"^1.0"` | Compatible with 1.0 (>=1.0.0, <2.0.0) |
| `~` | `"~3.12"` | Approximately 3.12 (>=3.12.0, <3.13.0) |
| `=` | `"=1.2.3"` | Exact match |

### `[hooks]` — Custom Scripts

```toml
[hooks]
pre_setup = "echo 'Starting...'"
post_setup = "echo 'Done'"

[hooks.node]
post_install = "npm install -g typescript"

[hooks.config]
shell = "bash"            # bash | zsh | sh | fish | powershell
timeout = 300             # seconds
continue_on_error = false
```

Hook env vars: `JARVY_TOOL`, `JARVY_VERSION`, `JARVY_OS`, `JARVY_ARCH`, `JARVY_HOME`.

### `[env]` — Environment Variables

```toml
[env.vars]
NODE_ENV = "development"
EDITOR = "vim"
API_URL = { value = "http://localhost:3000", description = "Backend API" }

[env.secrets]
API_KEY = { env = "MY_API_KEY", required = true }
DB_PASSWORD = { from_file = "~/.secrets/db_pass" }

[env.config]
shell = "zsh"
update_rc = false
generate_dotenv = true
dotenv_path = ".env"
add_to_gitignore = false
backup_rc = true
```

### `[roles]` — Role-Based Configurations

```toml
role = "frontend"
# or
role = ["frontend", "devops"]

[roles.base]
description = "Base development tools"
tools = ["git", "docker"]

[roles.frontend]
extends = "base"
description = "Frontend development"
tools = ["node", "bun"]

[roles.frontend.tools]
node = "20"
bun = "latest"

[roles.senior-frontend]
extends = "frontend"     # Max 5 levels of inheritance
tools = ["kubectl"]
```

Direct `[provisioner]` always overrides role tools. Use `--role <name>` to override role for a single setup run.

### `[services]` — Docker Compose / Tilt

```toml
[services]
enabled = true
auto_start = true
compose_file = "docker-compose.yml"
tilt_file = "Tiltfile"
start_in_ci = false
```

### `[network]` — Proxy and TLS

```toml
[network]
http_proxy = "http://proxy.corp.com:8080"
https_proxy = "http://proxy.corp.com:8080"
all_proxy = "socks5://socks.corp.com:1080"
no_proxy = ["localhost", "127.0.0.1", ".corp.com"]

[network.auth]
username = "jdoe"
password = { env = "PROXY_PASSWORD" }

[network.tls]
ca_bundle = "/etc/ssl/certs/corporate-ca.crt"
insecure = false

[network.overrides.git]
https_proxy = "http://git-proxy.corp.com:8888"
```

Propagated env vars: `HTTP_PROXY`, `HTTPS_PROXY`, `ALL_PROXY`, `NO_PROXY` (both cases), `CURL_CA_BUNDLE`, `SSL_CERT_FILE`, `NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS`, `GIT_SSL_CAINFO`, `REQUESTS_CA_BUNDLE`.

### `[git]` — Git Configuration

```toml
[git]
user_name = "Jane Doe"
user_email = { env = "GIT_EMAIL", default = "jane@example.com" }

signing = true
signing_key = "~/.ssh/id_ed25519.pub"
signing_format = "ssh"            # ssh | gpg, auto-detected from extension

default_branch = "main"
pull_rebase = true
auto_stash = true
push_autosetup = true
editor = "vim"

autocrlf = "input"                # true | false | input
eol = "lf"

credential_helper = "osxkeychain"

scope = "global"                  # global | local

[git.aliases]
co = "checkout"
br = "branch"
ci = "commit"
st = "status"
lg = "log --oneline --graph --decorate"
```

`ConfigValue` resolution: plain string, `{ env = "VAR" }`, or `{ env = "VAR", default = "..." }`.

Credential helper defaults: macOS=`osxkeychain`, Linux=`cache`, Windows=`manager-core`.

### Language Packages — `[npm]`, `[pip]`, `[cargo]`

```toml
[npm]
typescript = "^5.0"
eslint = "latest"
package_manager = "pnpm"     # auto-detected from lock file if omitted
from_lockfile = false

[pip]
pytest = ">=7.0"
black = "latest"
venv = ".venv"
create_venv = true
from_lockfile = false

[cargo]
cargo-watch = "latest"
cargo-nextest = "0.9"
locked = true
```

Detailed package spec:

```toml
[cargo]
some-crate = { version = "1.0", features = ["feat1"], locked = true }
```

npm package manager auto-detect: `pnpm-lock.yaml`→pnpm, `yarn.lock`→yarn, `bun.lockb`→bun, `package-lock.json`→npm.

### `[drift]` — Drift Detection

```toml
[drift]
enabled = true
check_on_run = false
track_files = [".vscode/settings.json", "package.json"]
version_policy = "minor"     # major | minor | patch | exact
ignore_tools = ["vim"]
allow_upgrades = true
```

State written to `.jarvy/state.json` after successful setup.

### `[logging]` — File Logging

```toml
[logging]
enabled = true
level = "info"           # error | warn | info | debug | trace
format = "json"          # text | json
max_file_size = "10MB"
max_files = 5
max_age_days = 30
```

Log path: `~/.jarvy/logs/jarvy.log` (rotated `.1.gz`, `.2.gz`, …).

### `[workspace]` — Monorepos

```toml
[workspace]
members = ["services/api", "services/web", "libs/shared"]
inherit = ["provisioner", "hooks", "env"]
```

### `[privileges]`

```toml
[privileges]
use_sudo = true

[privileges.per_os]
linux = true
macos = false
windows = false
```

### `[commands]` — Interactive Menu

```toml
[commands]
run = "npm start"
test = "npm test"
setup = "jarvy setup"
```

### `extends` — Config Inheritance

```toml
extends = "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/org/configs/main/base.toml"
# or
extends = "../shared/base-jarvy.toml"
```

---

## 4. CLI Reference

### Core

```bash
jarvy setup [-f file] [--from URL] [--role NAME] [--dry-run]
            [--no-hooks] [--ci|--no-ci] [-j N] [--sequential]
            [--ignore-missing-deps] [-q|-v]

jarvy init [--template NAME] [--non-interactive] [--stdout]

jarvy doctor [--tools T1,T2] [--extended] [--format json] [--report PATH]

jarvy diff [--changes-only] [--format json]
```

### Discovery

```bash
jarvy search <query>                # Fuzzy search
jarvy search --all                  # List all tools
jarvy explain <tool> [--file F] [--format json]
jarvy tools [--index] [--default-hooks] [--format json]
```

### Configuration

```bash
jarvy validate [--from URL] [--strict]
jarvy migrate [--apply] [--format json]
jarvy export [--all] [--tools T1,T2] [--format json]
jarvy configure                     # Generate default jarvy.toml
jarvy schema [--output PATH]        # JSON Schema for jarvy.toml
```

### Roles

```bash
jarvy roles list [-v]
jarvy roles show <name> [--resolved] [--inheritance]
jarvy roles diff <a> <b>
```

### Team

```bash
jarvy team list
jarvy team add <url>
jarvy team remove <name>
```

### Environment

```bash
jarvy env [--dotenv] [--shell] [--dry-run] [--export]
```

### Drift

```bash
jarvy drift check [--format json]
jarvy drift status [-v]
jarvy drift accept [--tools T1,T2]
jarvy drift fix [--dry-run]
```

Exit codes: 0=no drift, 1=drift detected, 2=no baseline.

### Security

```bash
jarvy audit [--tool NAME] [--format json]
# Scanners: betterleaks, gitleaks, trufflehog, trivy, grype, semgrep, checkov, tfsec
```

### CI/CD

```bash
jarvy ci-config <provider> [--dry-run]
# Providers: github, gitlab, circleci, azure, jenkins, bitbucket
jarvy ci-info
```

### Self-Update

```bash
jarvy update [--version V] [--channel C] [--rollback]
jarvy update check
jarvy update history
jarvy update config
jarvy update enable | disable
```

Env: `JARVY_UPDATE=0`, `JARVY_UPDATE_CHANNEL=beta`, `JARVY_PINNED_VERSION=1.2.3`.

### Maintenance

```bash
jarvy upgrade [--tools T1,T2] [--dry-run]
jarvy diagnose <tool> [--fix]
```

### Logging & Tickets

```bash
jarvy logs view [--lines N] [--level L]
jarvy logs stats
jarvy logs clean [--all]
jarvy logs config

jarvy ticket create [--tool NAME]
jarvy ticket show <id>
jarvy ticket list
jarvy ticket clean [--older-than DAYS]
```

### Telemetry

```bash
jarvy telemetry status
jarvy telemetry enable | disable
jarvy telemetry set-endpoint <url>
jarvy telemetry test
jarvy telemetry preview
```

Env: `JARVY_TELEMETRY=1`, `JARVY_OTLP_ENDPOINT`, `JARVY_OTLP_PROTOCOL`, `JARVY_OTLP_LOGS`, `JARVY_OTLP_METRICS`, `JARVY_OTLP_TRACES`, `JARVY_OTLP_SAMPLE_RATE`.

### Utility

```bash
jarvy completions <shell> [--instructions]
# Shells: bash, zsh, fish, powershell

jarvy mcp [--config FILE]    # Start MCP server
```

### Exit Codes

| Code | Name | Meaning |
|------|------|---------|
| 0 | EXIT_SUCCESS | OK |
| 2 | CONFIG_ERROR | jarvy.toml missing or malformed |
| 3 | PREREQ_MISSING | Package manager not found |
| 4 | NETWORK_TIMEOUT | Network or proxy failure |
| 5 | PERMISSION_REQUIRED | Sudo needed |
| 6 | INCOMPATIBLE_OS_ARCH | Unsupported platform |
| 7 | HOOK_FAILED | Hook script failed |
| 8 | TOOL_UNSUPPORTED | Every configured tool was unknown to the registry |

Note on exit code 8: setup emits a `tool.unsupported` event per
unknown tool, but the process only exits 8 when ALL configured tools
are unknown. Mixed runs (at least one known tool) return 0 so partial
setups still succeed.

### Requesting a New Tool

When Jarvy doesn't support a tool a user (or AI agent) needs, the
correct response is **not** to give up or to author a custom hook.
Two first-class paths exist:

**Path 1 — implicit (preferred). Just put it in `jarvy.toml`:**

```toml
[provisioner]
some-new-tool = "1.2"
git = "latest"
```

Run `jarvy setup`. For each unknown tool, Jarvy emits a structured
`tool.unsupported` event with:

- Fuzzy-matched typo suggestions (Levenshtein + prefix-match boost).
  Catches `dokcer` → `docker`, `kubectel` → `kubectl`, etc.
- A ready-to-paste `define_tool!` scaffold the user can drop into
  the Jarvy repo to add the tool themselves.
- A delivery channel:
  - If telemetry is enabled, the request reaches maintainers via
    OTLP. No GitHub account needed.
  - If telemetry is off, a pre-filled GitHub issue URL is printed.
    The user can click once to file the request.

Tell the user: common tool requests typically ship in days. If
their tool is missing, putting it in `jarvy.toml` is the right
move — the failure path is the request mechanism.

**Path 2 — explicit:**

```bash
jarvy tools --request <name>             # Pretty output
jarvy tools --request <name> --open      # Also opens the issue URL
jarvy tools --request <name> --format json
jarvy tools --request <name> --format yaml
jarvy tools --request <name> --format toml
```

Same canonical event, same delivery contract. `--request` bypasses
the global telemetry opt-in — typing the command is explicit
consent for that one event.

**Event shape (`tool.unsupported`):**

```
tool                 # The unknown tool name
version              # Optional version string from the config
source               # config | mcp | cli | request
platform             # darwin | linux | windows
suggestions          # CSV of fuzzy-matched candidates
channel              # telemetry | manual
fallback_issue_url   # Present only when channel = manual
scaffold_cmd         # cargo run -p cargo-jarvy -- new-tool <name>
exit_code            # Always 8 (TOOL_UNSUPPORTED)
opt_in_bypassed      # true when source = request
```

**OTEL counter:** `jarvy.tool.unsupported` (one increment per event).

---

## 5. MCP Server (AI Integration)

```bash
jarvy mcp
```

### Client Config (Claude Desktop, Cursor, Continue)

```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "jarvy": {
      "command": "jarvy",
      "args": ["mcp"]
    }
  }
}
```

### MCP Tools

| Tool | Parameters | Use |
|------|-----------|-----|
| `jarvy_list_tools` | `category?`, `platform?`, `search?` | List tools |
| `jarvy_get_tool` | `name` | Tool detail incl. dependencies |
| `jarvy_check_tool` | `name` | Is it installed? |
| `jarvy_check_multiple` | `tools[]` | Bulk check |
| `jarvy_install_tool` | `name`, `version?`, `dry_run?` | Install (dry_run=true default) |

### MCP Resources

- `jarvy://tools/index` — full tool catalog
- `jarvy://platform/info` — host info
- `jarvy://tools/{name}` — per-tool detail

### MCP Prompts

- `setup_dev_environment` — `project_type` arg
- `diagnose_missing_tools`

### Safety

```toml
# ~/.jarvy/mcp-config.toml
[mcp]
require_confirmation = true
default_dry_run = true

[rate_limits]
checks_per_minute = 10
installs_per_minute = 3

[allowlist]
tools = ["git", "node", "python"]

# [denylist]
# tools = ["dangerous-tool"]

[audit]
enabled = true
log_path = "~/.jarvy/mcp-audit.log"
```

### MCP Error Codes

| Code | Meaning |
|------|---------|
| -32001 | Tool execution failed |
| -32002 | Tool not found |
| -32003 | Installation denied (denylist) |
| -32004 | Rate limited |
| -32005 | User declined |
| -32006 | Sudo required |
| -32007 | Timeout |
| -32008 | Missing dependency |

Protocol version targeted: `2024-11-05`.

---

## 6. Tool Dependencies

Two kinds:

- **Strict** (`depends_on`): ALL listed tools required
- **Flexible** (`depends_on_one_of`): AT LEAST ONE required

Examples:

| Tool | Strict | Flexible |
|------|--------|----------|
| lazydocker | docker | – |
| kind | docker | – |
| kubectl | – | minikube, kind, k3d, docker, podman |
| helm | – | kubectl |
| k9s | – | kubectl |
| minikube | – | docker, podman |
| dive | – | docker, podman |

Jarvy topologically sorts tools by dependencies during install. Missing strict deps trigger warnings but don't block.

---

## 7. CI/CD

### GitHub Actions

```yaml
jobs:
  setup:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: bearbinary/jarvy/.github/actions/setup-jarvy@main
        with:
          method: cargo
      - run: jarvy setup --ci
```

`--ci` enables non-interactive mode. Auto-detected providers: GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, CircleCI, Azure DevOps, Jenkins, Bitbucket Pipelines, Travis, Drone, Buildkite, Codefresh, Semaphore (11 total).

### CI Detection

`CI=true` → non-interactive mode. Telemetry and update checks auto-disable in CI unless overridden.

---

## 8. Architecture (For Code-Modifying Agents)

### Module Map

```
src/main.rs                Entry point (~540 lines)
src/cli/                   clap definitions
  args.rs                  Cli, Commands, OutputFormat
  subcommands.rs           Nested action enums
src/commands/              One file per top-level command
src/config.rs              TOML schema + validation
src/remote.rs              Remote config fetch + cache
src/roles/                 Role-based config inheritance
  definition.rs            Types
  resolver.rs              Inheritance resolver
  commands.rs              CLI surface
src/tools/                 Tool registry
  spec.rs                  ToolSpec + define_tool! macro
  registry.rs              OnceLock<RwLock<HashMap>>
  common.rs                Os, InstallError, run/has helpers
  <name>/<name>.rs         Per-tool definitions
src/packages/              Language packages
  npm.rs, pip.rs, cargo_pkg.rs
src/git/                   Git config automation
src/network/               Proxy, TLS, env propagation
src/drift/                 Drift detection
src/update/                Self-update
src/logging/               File logging
src/ticket/                Debug bundles
src/services/              Docker Compose + Tilt
src/env/                   Env vars + dotenv
src/team/                  Shared team configs
src/lock/                  Tool version locking
src/mcp/                   MCP server
src/telemetry.rs           OTEL pipeline
src/observability/         Telemetry helpers
src/error_codes.rs         Exit code constants
build.rs                   Generates tool index JSON
```

### Tool Definition (`define_tool!`)

```rust
use crate::define_tool;

define_tool!(JQ, {
    command: "jq",
    macos: { brew: "jq" },
    linux: { uniform: "jq" },
    windows: { winget: "jqlang.jq" },
});
```

Variants:

- `macos: { brew: "pkg" }` — Homebrew formula
- `macos: { cask: "pkg" }` — Homebrew cask (GUI apps)
- `linux: { uniform: "pkg" }` — Same name across distros
- `linux: { apt: "x", dnf: "y", pacman: "z", apk: "w" }` — Per-manager
- `windows: { winget: "Pub.Pkg" }` — Winget package ID
- `custom_install: Some(fn_name)` — For shell-script installs (nvm, rustup)
- `default_hook: { description: "...", script: "..." }` — Auto post-install hook
- `depends_on: &["tool1", "tool2"]` — Strict deps
- `depends_on_one_of: &["tool1", "tool2"]` — Flexible deps

### Setup Flow

1. Parse args (clap)
2. Init telemetry (OTEL)
3. Load config + resolve `extends`
4. Apply role inheritance
5. Build install plan, topologically sort by deps
6. Pre-setup hook
7. Parallel tool install (rayon, default 4 jobs)
8. Per-tool post_install hooks
9. Default hooks (tool-defined)
10. Language packages (npm/pip/cargo)
11. Git config
12. Env vars + dotenv
13. Services start
14. Post-setup hook
15. Drift baseline write

### Test Env Vars

- `JARVY_TEST_MODE=1` — disable interactive prompts
- `JARVY_FAST_TEST=1` — skip external command execution

### Verification Before Commit

```bash
cargo fmt --all
cargo clippy --all-features -- -D warnings
cargo check --verbose
cargo test --verbose -- --show-output
```

CI gates: clippy `correctness=deny`, suspicious/perf/style/complexity `warn`. Edition 2024, rustc ≥ 1.85.

### Conventional Commits

`feat:`, `fix:`, `docs:`, `chore:`, `refactor:`, `test:`.

---

## 9. Telemetry

OpenTelemetry (OTLP) — logs, metrics, optional traces. Opt-in. Auto-disabled in CI unless `JARVY_TELEMETRY=1`.

Metrics emitted:
- `jarvy.command.count` (counter)
- `jarvy.tool.install.duration` (histogram)
- `jarvy.tool.install.count` (counter)
- `jarvy.setup.duration` (histogram)
- `jarvy.config.tool_count` (gauge)

No secrets, no file paths, no hostnames sent. Stable per-machine ID via `machineid-rs`.

---

## 10. Files and Locations

| Path | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `jarvy.toml` | Project config (committed) |
| `.jarvy/state.json` | Drift baseline (commit or gitignore based on team policy) |
| `~/.jarvy/config.toml` | Global Jarvy config (telemetry, update channel) |
| `~/.jarvy/logs/jarvy.log` | Persistent logs |
| `~/.jarvy/tickets/` | Debug bundles |
| `~/.jarvy/backups/` | Pre-update binary backups |
| `~/.jarvy/mcp-config.toml` | MCP server config |
| `~/.jarvy/mcp-audit.log` | MCP operation audit log |

---

## 11. Common Patterns

### Team config with role separation

```toml
role = "frontend"

[provisioner]
git = "latest"
docker = "latest"

[roles.base]
tools = ["git", "docker"]

[roles.frontend]
extends = "base"
tools = ["node", "bun", "typescript"]

[roles.backend]
extends = "base"
tools = ["go", "python", "kubectl"]
```

### Personal info kept out of shared config

```toml
[git]
user_name = "Jane Doe"
user_email = { env = "GIT_EMAIL" }
```

### Project bootstrap

```toml
[provisioner]
node = "20"
python = "3.12"

[npm]
typescript = "^5.0"
eslint = "latest"

[pip]
pytest = ">=7.0"
black = "latest"
venv = ".venv"

[hooks.node]
post_install = "corepack enable"
```

### CI gate against drift

```yaml
- run: jarvy setup --ci
- run: jarvy drift check --format json
```

### Behind a corporate proxy

```toml
[network]
https_proxy = "http://proxy.corp.com:8080"
no_proxy = ["localhost", ".corp.com"]

[network.tls]
ca_bundle = "/etc/ssl/certs/corporate-ca.crt"
```

---

## 12. Anti-Patterns

- Putting secrets in `jarvy.toml` (use `{ env = "VAR" }`)
- Pinning every tool to `"latest"` (pin majors at minimum)
- Bypassing roles with multiple `jarvy.toml` files (model with `[roles.X]`)
- Redefining tools that exist (run `jarvy search` first)
- Scraping human-readable CLI output (use `--format json`)
- Disabling MCP rate limits (they prevent agent runaway loops)

---

## 13. Project Templates

Drop-in `jarvy.toml` templates live at `examples/<name>/jarvy.toml` in the
repo. Pick by detecting lockfiles in the user's project.

| Template | Detect by | Stack |
|----------|-----------|-------|
| `node-npm` | `package-lock.json` only | Node.js + npm |
| `node-pnpm` | `pnpm-lock.yaml` | Node.js + pnpm |
| `node-bun` | `bun.lockb` or `bunfig.toml` | Bun + node fallback |
| `deno` | `deno.json` / `deno.jsonc` | Deno |
| `python-api` | `requirements.txt` | Python + pip + venv |
| `python-uv` | `uv.lock` | Python + uv |
| `go-api` | `go.mod` + HTTP service | Go + air + goose + golangci-lint |
| `rust-cli` | `Cargo.toml` single-crate | Rust CLI |
| `rust-workspace` | `Cargo.toml` with `[workspace]` | Rust multi-crate |
| `ruby-rails` | `Gemfile` + Rails | Ruby on Rails + Postgres + Redis |
| `java-spring` | `pom.xml` w/ spring-boot parent | Java + Spring Boot |

Fetch one:

```bash
curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/bearbinary/jarvy/main/examples/<name>/jarvy.toml -o jarvy.toml
```

Each template ships `[provisioner]`, `[<lang-pkg>]`, `[hooks]`, `[env.vars]`,
`[commands]`, `[drift]`. See https://jarvy.dev/templates-index/ for the
full decision table.

---
